source.title: Evaluation of modafinil as a perpetrator of metabolic drug-drug interactions using a model informed cocktail reaction phenotyping trial protocol
source.finding: Modafinil is a borderline moderate CYP2C19 inhibitor. Omeprazole AUC increased 77% with modafinil co-administration. CYP2C19 poor metabolizers face compounded risk when taking modafinil alongside CYP2C19 substrates.

AA.phenotype: Poor Metabolizer (*2/*2) — High Interaction Risk with Modafinil
AA.effect: Already minimal CYP2C19 activity. Modafinil's CYP2C19 inhibition compounds the deficit. Co-administered CYP2C19 substrates (omeprazole, clopidogrel, diazepam, phenytoin, SSRIs) may reach dangerously high levels.
AA.recommendation: Avoid co-administering modafinil with CYP2C19 substrates. If unavoidable, reduce substrate dose and monitor closely. Consider alternative to clopidogrel (prasugrel/ticagrelor).

AG.phenotype: Intermediate Metabolizer (*1/*2) — Moderate Interaction Risk with Modafinil
AG.effect: Reduced CYP2C19 activity further inhibited by modafinil. CYP2C19 substrates may accumulate.
AG.recommendation: Monitor CYP2C19 substrate levels when co-administered with modafinil. Dose adjustments may be needed for omeprazole, diazepam, or SSRIs.

GG.phenotype: Normal Metabolizer (*1/*1) — Standard Interaction Risk with Modafinil
GG.effect: Normal CYP2C19 activity partially inhibited by modafinil (omeprazole AUC +77%). Clinically manageable in most cases.
GG.recommendation: Be aware of potential interaction with CYP2C19 substrates. Routine monitoring sufficient.
